title: BenchmarkSummary
description:
A `BenchmarkSummary` which contains all collected data of a single benchmark run

This is the top-level type most consumers work with after deserializing a Gungraun summary file.
Its fields describe the benchmark run itself, while `profiles` contains the collected metric
data, differences, and any `ToolRegression` values.

The `module_path` together with the `id` can serve as a unique identifier of a benchmark run. If
the `id` is not present then the unique identifier is just the `module_path`.

baselines.description:
The baselines if any.

An absent first baseline indicates that new output was produced. An absent second baseline
indicates the usage of the usual "*.old" output.

benchmark_exe.description:
The path to the binary which is executed by Gungraun and in turn Valgrind.

In case of a library benchmark this is the compiled benchmark file. In case of a binary
benchmark this is the path to the executable.

benchmark_file.description: The path to the file containing this benchmark

details.description: More details describing this benchmark run

function_name.description: The name of the function under test

id.description: The user provided id of this benchmark

kind.description: Whether this summary describes a library or binary benchmark

module_path.description: The rust path in the form `bench_file::group::bench`

profiles.description:
This is the container with all the benchmark data (metrics, differences, comparisons, ...)

If there were no errors during the benchmark run, there is at least one [`Profile`]
present.

summary_output.description: The destination and kind of the summary file

version.description:
The version string of this format.

This is not semver and only major version numbers are used. There might be text occurrences
of `v6` within this library documentation but v6 is stored as raw number `6` without the
`v` prefix. Only backwards incompatible changes cause an increase of the version

BenchmarkKind.description: Identifies whether a summary describes a library or binary benchmark.

oneOf.description: A library benchmark
A binary benchmark

CachegrindMetric.description:
Identifiers for Cachegrind metrics that can appear in a parsed summary.

This enum covers both raw Cachegrind events and Gungraun-derived values
such as hit rates and estimated cycles.

oneOf.description: The default event. I cache reads (which equals the number of instructions executed)
D Cache reads (which equals the number of memory reads) (--cache-sim=yes)
D Cache writes (which equals the number of memory writes) (--cache-sim=yes)
I1 cache read misses (--cache-sim=yes)
D1 cache read misses (--cache-sim=yes)
D1 cache write misses (--cache-sim=yes)
LL cache instruction read misses (--cache-sim=yes)
LL cache data read misses (--cache-sim=yes)
LL cache data write misses (--cache-sim=yes)

I1 cache miss rate (--cache-sim=yes)
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]

LL/L2 instructions cache miss rate (--cache-sim=yes)
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]

D1 cache miss rate (--cache-sim=yes)
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]

LL/L2 data cache miss rate (--cache-sim=yes)
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]

LL/L2 cache miss rate (--cache-sim=yes)
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]
Derived event showing the L1 hits (--cache-sim=yes)
Derived event showing the LL hits (--cache-sim=yes)
Derived event showing the RAM hits (--cache-sim=yes)

L1 cache hit rate (--cache-sim=yes)
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]

A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]
LL/L2 cache hit rate (--cache-sim=yes)

RAM hit rate (--cache-sim=yes)
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]
Derived event showing the total amount of cache reads and writes (--cache-sim=yes)
Derived event showing estimated CPU cycles (--cache-sim=yes)
Conditional branches executed (--branch-sim=yes)
Conditional branches mispredicted (--branch-sim=yes)
Indirect branches executed (--branch-sim=yes)
Indirect branches mispredicted (--branch-sim=yes)

DhatMetric.description: Identifiers for DHAT metrics that can appear in a parsed summary.

oneOf.description: In ad-hoc mode, Total units measured over the entire execution
Total ad-hoc events over the entire execution
Total bytes allocated over the entire execution
Total heap blocks allocated over the entire execution
The bytes alive at t-gmax, the time when the heap size reached its global maximum
The blocks alive at t-gmax

The amount of bytes at the end of the execution.

This is the amount of bytes which were not explicitly freed.

The amount of blocks at the end of the execution.

This is the amount of heap blocks which were not explicitly freed.
The amount of bytes read during the entire execution
The amount of bytes written during the entire execution
The total lifetimes of all heap blocks allocated
The maximum amount of bytes
The maximum amount of heap blocks

Diffs.description: Percentage and factor differences derived from two compared metric values.

diff_pct.description:
The percentage of the difference between two `Metrics` serialized as string to preserve
infinity values and avoid `null` in json

factor.description:
The factor of the difference between two `Metrics` serialized as string to preserve
infinity values and avoid `null` in json

EitherOrBoth.description: Represent values that have either a `Left` or `Right` value or `Both` values

oneOf.description: Represents a value from the left side
Represents a value from both sides
Represents a value from the right side

EitherOrBoth2.description: Represent values that have either a `Left` or `Right` value or `Both` values

oneOf.description: Represents a value from the left side
Represents a value from both sides
Represents a value from the right side

ErrorMetric.description:
Identifiers for the error counts reported by error-detecting Valgrind tools.

These values appear in parsed summaries for `Memcheck`, `Helgrind`, and `DRD`.

oneOf.description: The amount of detected unsuppressed errors
The amount of detected unsuppressed error contexts
The amount of suppressed errors
The amount of suppressed error contexts

EventKind.description:
Identifiers for Callgrind events that can appear in a parsed summary.

This enum includes both raw Callgrind events and Gungraun-derived values such as hit rates and
aggregate counts.

oneOf.description: The default event. I cache reads (which equals the number of instructions executed)
D Cache reads (which equals the number of memory reads) (--cache-sim=yes)
D Cache writes (which equals the number of memory writes) (--cache-sim=yes)
I1 cache read misses (--cache-sim=yes)
D1 cache read misses (--cache-sim=yes)
D1 cache write misses (--cache-sim=yes)
LL cache instruction read misses (--cache-sim=yes)
LL cache data read misses (--cache-sim=yes)
LL cache data write misses (--cache-sim=yes)

I1 cache miss rate (--cache-sim=yes).
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]

LL/L2 instructions cache miss rate (--cache-sim=yes)
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]

D1 cache miss rate (--cache-sim=yes)
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]

LL/L2 data cache miss rate (--cache-sim=yes)
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]

LL/L2 cache miss rate (--cache-sim=yes)
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]
Derived event showing the L1 hits (--cache-sim=yes)
Derived event showing the LL hits (--cache-sim=yes)
Derived event showing the RAM hits (--cache-sim=yes)

L1 cache hit rate (--cache-sim=yes)
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]

LL/L2 cache hit rate (--cache-sim=yes)
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]

RAM hit rate (--cache-sim=yes)
A [`Metric::Float`][crate::metrics::model::Metric]
Derived event showing the total amount of cache reads and writes (--cache-sim=yes)
Derived event showing estimated CPU cycles (--cache-sim=yes)
The number of system calls done (--collect-systime=yes)
The elapsed time spent in system calls (--collect-systime=yes)
The cpu time spent during system calls (--collect-systime=nsec)
The number of global bus events (--collect-bus=yes)
Conditional branches executed (--branch-sim=yes)
Conditional branches mispredicted (--branch-sim=yes)
Indirect branches executed (--branch-sim=yes)
Indirect branches mispredicted (--branch-sim=yes)
Dirty miss because of instruction read (--simulate-wb=yes)
Dirty miss because of data read (--simulate-wb=yes)
Dirty miss because of data write (--simulate-wb=yes)
Counter showing bad temporal locality for L1 caches (--cachuse=yes)
Counter showing bad temporal locality for LL caches (--cachuse=yes)
Counter showing bad spatial locality for L1 caches (--cachuse=yes)
Counter showing bad spatial locality for LL caches (--cachuse=yes)

FlamegraphSummary.description:
File paths for one flamegraph associated with a specific [`EventKind`].

At least one of `regular_path`, `base_path`, or `diff_path` is present.

base_path.description: If present, the path to the file of the old regular (non-differential) flamegraph

diff_path.description: If present, the path to the file of the differential flamegraph

regular_path.description: If present, the path to the file of the regular (non-differential) flamegraph

Metric.description:
The value type used for metrics measured by a benchmark tool

Raw metrics emitted by Valgrind tools are [`Metric::Int`] which is the default metric type.
Metrics that have [`Metric::Float`] type are documented as such. Derived values, such as miss
rates and hit rates, require floating-point representation. `Metric` preserves both forms in the
parsed summary model.

# Developer Notes

Float operations with a `Metric` that stores a `u64` introduce a precision loss and are to be
avoided. Especially comparison between a `u64` metric and `f64` metric are not exact because the
`u64` has to be converted to a `f64`. Also, if adding/multiplying two `u64` metrics would result
in an overflow the metric saturates at `u64::MAX`. This choice was made to preserve precision
and the original type (instead of for example adding the two `u64` by converting both of them to
`f64`).

oneOf.description: An integer `Metric`
A float `Metric`

MetricKind.description:
Identifies a metric kind by tool

This enum appears in places where a summary needs to describe a metric without separately
carrying the tool family that owns it.

oneOf.description: The `None` kind if there are no metrics for a tool (i.e. BBV and Massif)
The Callgrind metric kind
The Cachegrind metric kind
The DHAT metric kind
The Memcheck metric kind
The Helgrind metric kind
The DRD metric kind

MetricsDiff.description:
Comparison data for one metric in a parsed summary.

If both, old and new values, are present, [`Diffs`] stores the derived percentage and factor.
Otherwise the summary only stores whichever side is available. Per convention, the left side or
[`EitherOrBoth::Left`] stores the new [`Metric`] and the right side or [`EitherOrBoth::Right`]
stores the old metric.

diffs.description: If both metrics ([`EitherOrBoth::Both`]) are present there is also a `Diffs` present

MetricsSummary.description: An insertion-ordered mapping from metric identifier to [`MetricsDiff`].

MetricsSummary2.description: An insertion-ordered mapping from metric identifier to [`MetricsDiff`].

MetricsSummary3.description: An insertion-ordered mapping from metric identifier to [`MetricsDiff`].

MetricsSummary4.description: An insertion-ordered mapping from metric identifier to [`MetricsDiff`].

Profile.description: `Profile` data for one [`ValgrindTool`] recorded in a benchmark summary.

flamegraphs.description: Details and information about the created flamegraphs if any

log_paths.description: The paths to the `*.log` files. All tools produce at least one log file

out_paths.description:
The paths to the `*.out` files. Not all tools produce an output in addition to the log
files

summaries.description: The data with the metrics and details about the tool run

ProfileData.description:
All [`ProfilePart`]-level and [`ProfileTotal`] data of a single tool run.

The [`ProfileTotal`] is always present and summarizes all [`ProfilePart`]s. If the tool produced
only one part, the total matches that part's metrics.

ProfileInfo.description: Metadata describing a single [`ProfilePart`] of a benchmark

details.description: More details for example from the logging output of the tool run

parent_pid.description: The parent pid of this process if present

part.description: The part number of this tool run if present (only Callgrind)

path.description: The path to the output file containing the data of the tool run

pid.description: The pid of the benchmark process

thread.description: The thread number of this tool run if present (only Callgrind)

ProfilePart.description:
A single part of a tool run with the collected metric data

A tool run can produce multiple parts, for example one per process when child tracing is
enabled.

details.description: [`ProfileInfo`] like command, pid, ppid, thread number etc.

ProfileTotal.description: Aggregated metrics, differences and regressions over all parts of a tool run.

summary.description: The [`ToolMetricSummary`] of the tool containing the collected metric data

Profiles.description: Contains all [`Profile`]s with the data for each [`ValgrindTool`] run

SummaryFormat.description: Identifies the format of a summary file written by Gungraun.

oneOf.description: The format in a space optimal json representation without newlines
The format in pretty printed json

SummaryOutput.description: Describes where Gungraun wrote the summary file and in which format.

path.description: The path to the destination file of this summary

oneOf.description: If there are no metrics extracted (currently Massif, BBV)
The summary of tools which report errors (Memcheck, Helgrind, DRD) ([`ErrorMetric`])
The metric summary of [`DhatMetric`]s
The Callgrind summary of [`EventKind`]
The summary of [`CachegrindMetric`]s

ToolRegression.description:
A regression detected while evaluating a [`BenchmarkSummary`].

Soft regressions compare a new value against an older measurement using a percentage threshold.
Hard regressions compare a new value against an absolute limit.

oneOf.description: A performance regression triggered by a soft limit

diff_pct.description:
The difference between new and old in percent. Serialized as string to preserve
infinity values and avoid null in json.

limit.description:
The value of the limit which was exceeded to cause a performance regression. Serialized
as string to preserve infinity values and avoid null in json.

new.description: The [`Metric`] value of the new benchmark run

old.description: The [`Metric`] value of the old benchmark run

oneOf.description: A performance regression triggered by a hard limit

diff.description: The difference between new and the limit as [`Metric`]

new.description: The [`Metric`] value of the benchmark run

ValgrindTool.description: The Valgrind tools which can be run in a benchmark

oneOf.description:
Callgrind: a call-graph generating cache and branch prediction profiler
<https://valgrind.org/docs/manual/cl-manual.html>

Cachegrind: a high-precision tracing profiler
<https://valgrind.org/docs/manual/cg-manual.html>

DHAT: a dynamic heap analysis tool
<https://valgrind.org/docs/manual/dh-manual.html>

Memcheck: a memory error detector
<https://valgrind.org/docs/manual/mc-manual.html>

Helgrind: a thread error detector
<https://valgrind.org/docs/manual/hg-manual.html>

DRD: a thread error detector
<https://valgrind.org/docs/manual/drd-manual.html>

Massif: a heap profiler
<https://valgrind.org/docs/manual/ms-manual.html>

BBV: an experimental basic block vector generation tool
<https://valgrind.org/docs/manual/bbv-manual.html>
